title: Lesson 7 Overview
subtitle: Listen to the lesson explained in English

transcript.en: Four chunks today. Modes of **transport**, what you're up to this **weekend**, the four **seasons**, and a tiny but powerful set of words called **particles**. Let's go!
16 ways to move around. Most are familiar — Maltese borrowed loads from Italian and English.
Most plurals just **add -i** at the end. Karozza → karozzi. Mutur → muturi. Easy.
But some are weirder. **Vann** becomes **vannijiet**. **Dgħajsa** becomes **dgħajjes**. Don't fight it — listen and repeat. 🎯
All these phrases start with two magic words:
Means **'I'm going to'** — and you just add the activity. 🎯
I'm going to a village festa:
Memorise 'Se mmur' and you can plug in any activity. 💪
Notice the article on each season — **Ir-, Is-, Il-, Ix-**. Every form of 'the' from Lesson 1 in action.
Particles are **tiny words that connect things** — like 'with', 'of', 'in', 'until'.
11 to learn. Most look short and odd, but each does a lot of work.
These are **on their own** — no article. In Lesson 8 you'll see what happens when they MEET the article. Spoiler: they fuse together. 🪄
Transport. Weekend plans. Seasons. Particles. That's lesson 7.
Tap a section, drill, repeat. Two more to go. 💪
